title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series Ep 46: How to Upscale Your AI Images (Update)
description:
Welcome to Episode 46 of the ComfyUI Tutorial Series! In this episode, we're revisiting AI image upscaling with improved workflows that eliminate common issues like vertical bands and artifacts—especially when using Flux and SDXL models.

You’ll learn step-by-step how to:
- Upscale your AI-generated images with Flux, SDXL, and Stable Diffusion 1.5
- Use the Tiled Diffusion node to avoid upscaling artifacts
- Optimize workflows based on your VRAM limits
- Install essential custom nodes like Easy Use, RG Three, Tiled Diffusion, and GGUF
- Compare two upscaling outputs: one with diffusion for quality and another simple upscale for larger size
- Run workflows on local machines or cloud platforms like RunningHub

Get the workflows and instructions from discord for free. Look for the pixaroma-workflows channel at episode 46
https://discord.gg/gggpkVgBf3

Flux Upscaler in the Cloud on RunningHub
https://www.runninghub.ai/post/1920487419039121410?utm_source=kol02-RH003

Check Other Episodes
ht
transcript: Welcome to episode 46 of our Comfy UI Tutorial series.  Today, I'm showing you how to upscale your AI generated images. For each model, the denoys is different.  For this one, a value between 0.2 and 0.4 works fine.  Use 0.2 to keep it more similar to your image,  and go higher to make it more creative.  After that, the image is decoded,  and I added a clear VRAM, just in case,  then the image is saved.  The size that enters the K-sampler is the final size that the image will be generated at.
